# terrible_amazon_aws_lambda (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The name you want to assign to the function you are uploading. Cannot be changed.

### Optional

- `architecture` (String) The instruction set architecture that the function supports. Requires one of `s3_bucket` or `zip_file`.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `dead_letter_arn` (String) The parent object that contains the target Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an Amazon SQS queue or Amazon SNS topic.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) A short, user-defined function description. Lambda does not use this value. Assign a meaningful description as you see fit.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `environment_variables` (String) A dictionary of environment variables the Lambda function is given.
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `handler` (String) The function within your code that Lambda calls to begin execution.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `image_uri` (String) The Amazon ECR URI of the image to use. Required (alternative to runtime zip_file and s3_bucket) when creating a function. Required when `state=present`.
- `kms_key_arn` (String) The KMS key ARN used to encrypt the function's environment variables.
- `layers` (String) A list of function layers to add to the function's execution environment. Specify each layer by its ARN, including the version.
- `memory_size` (Number) The amount of memory, in MB, your Lambda function is given.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `role` (String) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role that Lambda assumes when it executes your function to access any other Amazon Web Services (AWS) resources. You may use the bare ARN if the role belongs to the same AWS account. Required when `state=present`.
- `runtime` (String) The runtime environment for the Lambda function you are uploading. Required when creating a function. Uses parameters as described in boto3 docs. Required when `state=present`. For supported list of runtimes, see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/lambda-runtimes.html.
- `s3_bucket` (String) Amazon S3 bucket name where the .zip file containing your deployment package is stored. If `state=present` then either `zip_file` or `s3_bucket` must be present. `s3_bucket` and `s3_key` are required together.
- `s3_key` (String) The Amazon S3 object (the deployment package) key name you want to upload. `s3_bucket` and `s3_key` are required together.
- `s3_object_version` (String) The Amazon S3 object (the deployment package) version you want to upload.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Create or delete Lambda function.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `tracing_mode` (String) Set mode to 'Active' to sample and trace incoming requests with AWS X-Ray. Turned off (set to 'PassThrough') by default.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `vpc_security_group_ids` (String) List of VPC security group IDs to associate with the Lambda function. Required when `vpc_subnet_ids` is used.
- `vpc_subnet_ids` (String) List of subnet IDs to run Lambda function in. Use this option if you need to access resources in your VPC. Leave empty if you don't want to run the function in a VPC. If set, `vpc_security_group_ids` must also be set.
- `zip_file` (String) A .zip file containing your deployment package If `state=present` then either `zip_file` or `s3_bucket` must be present.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `code` (String) The lambda function's code returned by get_function in boto3.
- `configuration` (String) The Lambda function's configuration metadata returned by get_function in boto3.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
